publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-04-21 23:02:00 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Letters, diary fragments, notes, manuscripts, and other material documenting Burr's boyhood in Newark Valley, New York, and his student days at Cortland Academy, at Cornell University, and at Leipzig University; his travels and activities in Europe collecting rare books and manuscripts for Cornell; his relationship with Andrew Dickson White; his relationships with other American and European scholars and his students; his work for the Venezuela-Guiana Boundary Commission; and his interest in the American Historical Association, the Cornell Alumni Association, the Cornell Christian Association, the Hall of Fame at New York University, the Telluride Association, and in many other social and professional groups.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Professor of medieval history, librarian of the Andrew Dickson White Library at Cornell University.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Letters, diary fragments, notes, manuscripts, and other material documenting Burr's boyhood in Newark Valley, New York, and his student days at Cortland Academy, at Cornell University, and at Leipzig University; his travels and activities in Europe collecting rare books and manuscripts for Cornell; his relationship with Andrew Dickson White; his relationships with other American and European scholars and his students; his work for the Venezuela-Guiana Boundary Commission; and his interest in the American Historical Association, the Cornell Alumni Association, the Cornell Christian Association, the Hall of Fame at New York University, the Telluride Association, and in many other social and professional groups.

Correspondents include Lyman Abbott, Charles Kendall Adams, Roland H. Bainton, Carl Becker, Henry Bourne, James Bryce, John Burroughs, Anna and Henry Comstock, Reverend Robert Collyer, Leonard K. Elmhirst, Livingston Farrand, Max Farrand, Simon Henry Gage, Daniel Coit Gilman, Louis Gottschalk, Evarts B. Greene, Charles Gross, George Willam Harris, Albert Bushnell Hart, Charles Haskins, Ernest Huffcut, Charles Henry Hull, Edward M. Hulme, J. Franklin Jameson (mainly having to do with the AMERICAN HISTORICAL REVIEW), David Starr Jordan (extensive, quite impersonal), Louis C. Karpinski, Horace Kephart, Waldo G. Leland, John Bassett Moore, John R. Mott, David Saville Muzzey, Wallace Notestein, Cuthbert Pound, Herbert Putnam (Library of Congress), Jacob Gould Schurman, George H. Sabine, Goldwin Smith, Preserved Smith, H. Morse Stephens, Dorothy and Willard Straight, Alfred and Ernest Sze, Ida Tarbell, Frederick Jackson Turner, Moses Coit Tyler, Hendrik Willem Van Loon (extensive and intimate), Oswald Garrison Villard, Booker T. Washington, Benjamin Ide Wheeler, Frederick White, Burt G. Wilder, and many others.
Letter, 1903, from Andrew Dickson White about his trip to Elba, with comments about Napoleon I, and fourteen postcards, some annotated, of scenes of Elba.

emph.ref: Series II. Personal Documents and Cornell Material

list.item: Gladden, Washington, 1836-1918 [directed to Jacob Gould Schurman,*but in Burr Papers]

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: George Lincoln Burr papers, #14-17-22. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

odd.head: General
odd.p: *Neither the Burr nor Schurman Papers contain other Gladden letters for this period (Sept. - Oct. 1893, but the Schurman letterbooks contain a copy of one letter from Shcurman to Gladden re preaching at Sage, dated October 26, 1893

controlaccess.geogname: Newark Valley (N.Y.) -- Social life and customs.
controlaccess.subject: Librarians.
Historians.
College teachers.
Rare books.
Rare book libraries.
College students.
controlaccess.persname: Bourne, H. R. Fox (Henry Richard Fox), 1837-1909.
Napoleon, Emperor of the French, I, 1769-1821.
controlaccess.corpname: New York University. Hall of Fame for Great Americans

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including list of medallions, folder 5

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 4 letters from Horace Kephart to Burr, from Florence, Italy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 4 letters from Horace Kephart to Burr, from Florence, Italy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 1 letter from Horace Kephart to Burr, from Florence, Italy. Dated December 16.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 1 letter from Horace Kephart to Burr, from Florence, Italy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence regarding architectural photos

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence regarding the printing of the Reformation catalogue

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains original letter from AD White to Charles Kendall Adams regarding the gift of the Cornell Papyrus, dated March 18, 1889.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence on university photographs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters on Venezuela

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters on Venezuela and Blue Book

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters on Venezuela and Blue Book. Many addressed to Mallet-Prevost

scopecontent.p: Includes envelope of photos of Burr's family home

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters on Venezuela from Mallet-Prevost

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including notes on Saint Lucia, Republican campaign

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Related to the building of the new Chapel and Alumni Hall

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including correspondence on Venezuela

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including correspondence on trial of Maria Renata

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes mention of Professor Tyler's death

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes two letters from Hendrik Van Loon

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes two typescript copies of letters Hendrik Van Loon to E. Bell-Robinson

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including a letter and postcards from France, dates March 25.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including two letters from Win. A. Dunning, one about having Burr fill-in for a lecture in New Orleans and one on Giddings paper

scopecontent.p: Regarding publication of essays, especially Robertson's

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notestein's essay (cynical; anti-Semitic)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Regarding the management of the American Historical Reviews finances. Historian Frederic Bancroft got into an epistolary argument with GLB (treasurer). Burr later referred to this as the "Bancroft Episode."

did.unittitle: Win. A. Dunning to G.L. Burr

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Looking for new Columbia faculty member

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Appointment with Evans

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: recommending W.T. Morgan for Eng. History position

did.unittitle: R. Bainton & L.O. Gibbons Research Correspondence re G.L. Burr
Series II. Personal Documents and Cornell Material
Burr Family Material, G.L. Burr Pre-college Days
Burr family account book 1848-1856, followed by Burr's class notes and accounts with Cornell library
Burr's notebooks from Homer and Newark, New York
Book of handwritten poems, many by Mary Burr
Hepsy Burr's scrapbook, filled with obituaries and marriages and miscellaneous clippings
William J. Burr correspondence and writing book
Burr family genealogical papers, including sketches, biographies, family records pages from a Bible, and marriage records
Burr's pre-college notes, clippings, and memorabilia, mainly from Newark, Homer and Cortland
Burr's notebooks from lectures at Cornell
Class of 1881 Treasurer's Book, with notes and correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Annual Commencement Program 1879, Report of the Librarian, event tickets, Hill Jubilee program, Class Day Exercises 1878, Rules for Students 1878, Resolutions, The Library pamphlet 1883, Exercises for the Class of 1882 and 1879, and other printed pamphlets.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including Burr's oration "Revolt and Revolution"

did.unittitle: Burr's lecture notes and maps of North America
Book bibliography and memoranda, with small clippings from catalogues
Transcribed copy of Balthasar Hubmaier's "Von Letzern und ihren Verbrennen"
Collegien-buch from Leipzig, with diploma folded in the back
Note cards of lectures and notes
Title slips for books used at Basel and in the British Museum
Cornell Club pamphlet, Cornell invitations, and other Cornell items
Passports from the Department of State, with large watermarks
Class exams and notes, mainly from Germany
Burr's handwritten copies of "Politics in College" and "The Religious Philosophy of Lucretius"
Ithaca Congregational Church programs and pamphlets
Deposits in the White library, University library, and Christian Association library (incomplete)
Burr's application to military training camp
Pedigree form for Burke's Landed Gentry
Young Men's Youth Association event programs and pamphlets

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: From New York, Cornell, and Washington DC. Also includes Burr's notes on membership

did.unittitle: Cornell University Christian Association invitations and pamphlet

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes notes from a lecture Burr gave to C.U.C.A.

did.unittitle: Note cards on a lecture called "The Living Gospel", possibly given at the Unitarian Church
Notes, pamphlets, reports, and programs from the Unitarian Church

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes lecture "Talk on the Devil", "A Historical Development of the Christian Sunday", and "The Living Gospel

did.unittitle: Reports and copies of correspondence about the Red Cross
American Historical Association leaflets and pamphlets
Cornell Alumni Trustee nominations and biographies
Nicola Sacco and Bartolomeo Vanzetti trial leaflets
Clippings and lecture on the Fred Morelli case

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Fred Morelli, class of 1924, took a leave of absence after being attacked by classmates for not wearing his freshman cap. Burr vehemently opposed the use of force and spoke out against the attacks.

did.unittitle: Biographies and sketches of Cornell faculty and alumni
Burr's war and Civic Federation lecture notes
Pamphlets, publications and leaflets of events at other universities

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes invitations to events, program for "Alice and Wonderland", exam schedules, course listings, handwritten copy of "All Hal, Cornell!" by E.T. 1903, and petition to not take final exam.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes war relief brochures, leaflets on Austria aid, documents from the American Relief Administration (including 3 letters from recipients of Christmas food packages), outline for a handbook on relief work for students in Europe, and Cornell Rifle Club announcements

did.unittitle: Ramabai Circle of Cornell University treasurer's book with membership list and pamphlets
The Imprisonment of Ghent Professors, by Kr. Nyrop
Notes and clippings on Geology for Warfare of Science
Warfare of Science Notes, Mss., Revisions
Notes and copies of articles and speeches given by Burr on AD White
Notes on Charles Babcock, Bennett, Irving Church, Collyer, Comstock
Notes on Corson, Crane, Fiske, Hull, Russell, Schurman, Sill, Smith, Tyler, White
Talks by Burr, on White, Cornell, European Archives, and Venezuela

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes in binder

did.unittitle: 'New England's place in the History of Witchcraft' by Burr
"A Witch-Hunt in the Book Shops", by Burr
"The Historical Work of Henry C. Lea", by Burr
Burr's notes and drafts on introduction to Lea's materials
"Fragments from the sources- the Witchcraft Persecution" by Burr
"The Historical Work of Henry Charles Lea" by Burr
Kleiner Beitrag zur Geschichte des Hexenwesens, transcribed by Burr
Jan. 5, "1970 Accession : typed and printed items concerning the AHA-Bancroft episode, 1915; bibliographical notes (witchcraft).
George Lincoln Burr caricature in A.D. White library by Herbert Roland Bainton

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Formerly on display in the A.D. White library

did.unittitle: Cyanotype and blueprint of Sigma Phi and Professor Nichols house

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including Isabelle Robertson, Frances Boynton, Margaret Boynton, Mary Bartlett Hill, Emma Berry, Stella Spencer, Mrs. Clarke, Winifred Ball, and other Cornell students and associates

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including Lottie Stines, Martha Whilson, Fran Fanny Stadeli, Amelia Shapleigh, Emily Outwater, and others

did.unittitle: People, including Bobbie Stert, Winifred Ball and others

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including Anna Biedermann, Therese Biedermann, Mrs. Karl Biedermann, Ludwig Knorr, and history professors

did.unittitle: Photographs of the Burr's, labelled "to use" with chapter numbers and a sketch

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Taughannock, Cornell library (1890s), AE Kent and Co, Richmond from Manchester

did.unittitle: Telluride group photos, Cornell History Club,

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including Lyman Abbott, Evan Evans, Edward Huhur, Agnes Tierney

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including Isabel Howland, Harriet Day, Hannah Otis, Phebe Fort, Alice Goddard (1881), Clara Teague, Mary Putnam, and Gertrude van Pelt and others

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including FJ Scott (1880), FC Whitney, Frank Leverance, LF Boyer, George Schurman, Ostrander, Fred Kilborne and others

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including Herrick, Halsey, Hahn, Concklin, Curtice, Clarke, Wightman, Stearns, Holmes, Hoyt, Hough, Wing, David Halsey (1881), and Theobald Smith

did.unittitle: George Lincoln Burr in the AD White library and at his desk
Cabinet card photos of Burr taken at the Hague

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including Watkins Glen, Taughannock falls and point, Rocky point, South of Sheldrake, Burr on Cayuga Lake, Esty point

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including Mrs. Andrew Burr, Mrs. WJ Burr, WJ Burr, Sarah Burr, and unidentified

did.unittitle: Burr family home in Newark Valley and WJ Burr

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including Melville Landon, Harry Norman, Cornelia Smith, DE Roberts, Robina Smith, Albert Jonas, Jonah Walsh, Mary Roberts, and Mrs. RW Walsh
